CREATE TABLE test_class_structure (class_name VARCHAR(255), class_description XML);
SELECT class_name, JVM_REF_IMPORT(NULL, class_name) AS xml_description FROM test_class_structure WHERE class_name IN ('java.lang.Object', 'java.lang.System');
SELECT class_name FROM test_class_structure WHERE JVM_REF_IMPORT(NULL, class_name) IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE complex_data (id INT, attributes XML);
SELECT c.class_name, d.attributes FROM test_class_structure c JOIN complex_data d ON c.class_name = 'java.lang.String';
SELECT c.class_name FROM test_class_structure c WHERE JVM_REF_IMPORT(NULL, c.class_name) != JVM_REF_IMPORT(NULL, 'java.lang.Object');
CREATE TABLE nested_example (parent_id INT, child_data XML);
SELECT ne.parent_id, JVM_REF_IMPORT(NULL, 'java.util.Map') AS child_xml FROM nested_example ne WHERE parent_id = 1;
SELECT * FROM (SELECT class_name, JVM_REF_IMPORT(NULL, class_name) AS xml_description FROM test_class_structure WHERE class_name IS NOT NULL) AS inner_query WHERE xml_description IS NOT NULL;
CREATE TABLE multi_join_example (class1 VARCHAR(255), class2 VARCHAR(255), combined XML);

INSERT INTO multi_join_example SELECT a.class_name, b.class_name, (SELECT JVM_REF_IMPORT(NULL, a.class_name) || JVM_REF_IMPORT(NULL, b.class_name)) FROM test_class_structure a, test_class_structure b WHERE a.class_name = 'java.lang.Object' AND b.class_name = 'java.lang.System';
